metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated qualitative pain rather than a quantified business case: on the 25 Nov 2025 call, Nazual Azma Reffin said 'I think it's quite urgent now since we're supposed to migrate to kind of transaction,' citing manual tender handling as a bottleneck. Internally, Zycus discussed a ~US$50k-per-port target price (Avoma, 30 Dec 2025), later reframed as a group-wide play across all 7 MMC ports, but no ROI/savings figure has been validated with the buyer. Quantifying the savings case for the group-wide consolidation, tied to the budget-tolerance discussion now underway, is the key open move.

sources: SF Next_Step_History__c (10 Dec 2025, 29 Jan 2026, 12 Mar 2026 entries)
champion.narrative: Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus (Head of Tender and Contracts) was a strong, active champion through Q1 2026: per Next_Step, on 10 Dec 2025 she and her team were 'strongly leaning to Zycus, and anti-Ramco,' and on 29 Jan 2026 she secured management approval to move forward with Zycus modules. She was last explicitly referenced driving the deal around the 12 Mar 2026 Next_Step ('follow up our champion for close path'). Since the deal escalated to the group-wide MMC frame on 1 Jun 2026, she has not been mentioned in any Next_Step update or call, and the current activity is running through the group CEO and the MMC group owner's office instead. Her continued advocacy and access at the group level is unconfirmed, and she is at risk of being bypassed as the deal moves up.

sources: SF Next_Step_History__c (20 Jan, 29 Jan, 9 Feb 2026 entries)
competition.narrative: Ramco is the entrenched incumbent for P2P/purchasing and has actively fought to keep the account: Next_Step records it 'pushing them hard' on 20 Jan 2026, and Northport's management flip-flopped between retaining Ramco (29 Jan 2026) and dropping it (9 Feb 2026), with no confirmed resolution since. Competitors__c also lists iValua and SAP Ariba, but no call or Next_Step entry names either being actively evaluated by Northport or MMC; they carry no corroborating buyer evidence. The wedge against Ramco is e-bidding/tender capability plus AI-based contract authoring and scoring, which Ramco does not offer.

identify_pain.narrative: Northport's core pain is a manual, non-digitized tender/e-bidding process sitting alongside Ramco, which handles purchasing, supplier registration and renewals but has no e-bidding or tender capability. On the 25 Nov 2025 call, the buyer said 'unfortunately, it never materialized' regarding a prior Ramco/Zycus e-bidding collaboration, and asked Zycus directly: 'why don't we do for eBD... we want to see what you can offer.' A specific operational gap was also raised: tender-fee deposit payments require custom integration since suppliers currently pay and submit receipts manually. Pain owner: Northport's tender and procurement team.

paper_process.narrative: A concrete paper process existed briefly in Mar 2026: the 17 Mar 2026 S2P proposal, a phased-implementation revision on 30 Mar 2026, an LOI target of 31 Mar 2026, and a full BOM/MSA signature target of 7 Apr 2026 (with a 1% incremental discount offered per additional port brought in later). That target lapsed with no signature. Since the deal moved to the group-wide MMC frame on 1 Jun 2026, no contracting mechanics (MSA scope, legal review, procurement channel) have been re-established for the 7-port structure. Confirming what paper process applies once budget tolerance clears is unaddressed.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is now named at the highest level: the MMC group owner, per Next_Step (2 Jul 2026), who reportedly received the group-wide proposal with 'a positive response and level of interest.' A group-level budget/finance stakeholder (role, name unknown) is separately working internal channels to confirm budget tolerance across all 7 ports. However, neither has ever appeared on a Zycus-attended call; all engagement with them has been relayed through the local partner channel, so their authority and true sentiment remain unverified by Zycus directly. Securing a direct or partner-brokered confirmation from this office is the critical next step.

decision_process.narrative: The process has escalated through three tiers, visible in the call/Next_Step sequence: (1) Northport working-level (Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus, Nazual Azma Reffin, Agilaan Praemkumar) drove discovery and demo through Mar 2026; (2) Northport's 'new CEO' required Agilaan to present 2-3 price comparisons for sign-off before 6 Apr 2026; (3) as of 1 Jun 2026 the opportunity was merged into a group-wide MMC consolidation, taken to the group CEO (met 11 Jun 2026) and then to the MMC group owner (2 Jul 2026), with a group-level budget stakeholder now confirming tolerance across all 7 ports. This is a multi-tier approval chain that has moved decision authority progressively upward and away from the original Northport contacts.

decision_criteria.narrative: On the 11 Feb 2026 call the buyer confirmed they want the full procurement suite 'from MR through billing and payment with automated finance,' plus a supplier portal, and specifically asked for a guided hands-on workshop (90-120 minutes) covering the end-to-end Source-to-Pay suite with AI add-ons and ERP-agnostic integration mapping to the relevant stakeholder validation is also a stated criterion: the buyer asked for site visits to Zycus customers on Ramco ERP, and Carl Kimball named iRequest (16 subsidiaries, renewed 5 years), Telecom Malaysia, and Maybank (deep security review) as candidates.

items.name: Stage: Shortlisted → Formal Evaluation
items.summary: Deal moves forward into formal evaluation, but this follows a recent backslide from Vendor Selected to Shortlisted — suggesting the process restarted with wider scope rather than simply progressing.

items.name: Amount: 240,000 → 650,000
items.summary: Deal size nearly tripled, pointing to an expanded scope of the evaluation (e.g. more sites, modules, or users) rather than a like-for-like re-quote.

items.name: Forecast: Best Case → Pipeline
items.summary: Despite the stage and amount jump, forecast confidence was downgraded, indicating the bigger deal is seen as less certain to close — consistent with a reset evaluation rather than a straightforward upsell.

day_summary.overall: Big, contradictory day on paper: the deal advanced a stage to Formal Evaluation and the amount nearly tripled from 240K to 650K, but forecast was simultaneously downgraded from Best Case back to Pipeline — signalling this is a scope expansion still needing to be re-qualified, not a confirmed win. Combined with the prior slip from Vendor Selected back to Shortlisted and the close date pushed two months, the picture is a deal that got bigger but less certain, likely due to a re-scoped or re-opened evaluation on the buyer side. No inbound or outbound communication is recorded, so the driver behind these changes isn't documented — worth confirming what prompted the scope jump.

cro_panel.intro: A Formal Evaluation deal that has expanded from a single-port ~$240k proposal into a 7-port MMC group consolidation worth roughly $1M, with a positive but rep-reported signal from the group owner, while the buyer side has had no direct two-way call or email with Zycus in over three months and group budget confirmation and direct economic-buyer engagement remain outstanding

items.detail: Budget tolerance confirmation for the 7-port MMC group consolidation is pending (Next Step 2 Jul: 'Datuk Nural is now working channels to confirm budget tolerance for all 7 ports. Likely 1-2 weeks for responses'). The scope expanded from a Northport-only deal (~$240k) to a group-wide opportunity (~$150k/port x 7 = ~$1M), but the opp amount is still $650k and the budget is unconfirmed. The 30 Dec planning call noted budget constraints limited scope to core modules (iRisk and Anna held as optional). Without confirmed budget and EB sign-off, the deal cannot close.

The economic buyer is unmapped and unengaged. The Next Step names the MMC group owner and a Northport 'new CEO,' but neither is in contact roles or has appeared on calls. No CFO or finance executive is engaged on commercials, pricing, or ROI. The 12 Mar Next Step says 'internal discussion on next step (with CFO/CIO/CPO)' occurred, but it is unclear if this was a Zycus-attended meeting or internal buyer discussion, and no follow-up confirms EB engagement. Without EB sponsorship the deal cannot advance to the relevant stakeholder or close.

The buyer flip-flopped on the Ramco replacement decision: the 9 Feb Next Step states 'mgmt for Northport have decided to drop RAMCO now- flip flopped,' but the 29 Jan Next Step says 'mgmt is looking to retain RAMCO P2P, for short time while their contract expires.' The 20 Jan Next Step records 'Ramco pushing them hard (are aware to P2P side for them at risk).' The buyer's indecision and 3-month silence since 30 Mar suggest Ramco may have regained ground or internal alignment is unresolved.

The scope expanded from a Northport-only deal to a 7-port MMC group consolidation in June 2026 (Next Step 1 Jun: 'opportunity will move under a new entry for MMC- where we are trying to merge 7 ports and take a proposition directly to the owner'). This consolidation introduces multi-stakeholder approval complexity (budget tolerance checks across all 7 ports, group-level decision process) and decision delay. The opp amount ($650k) and stage (Formal Evaluation) do not reflect the expanded scope, and there is no evidence the consolidation has been formalized or the 7-port stakeholders aligned.

The original close target was 30 Mar 2026 (Next Step 6 Apr, 30 Mar entries); that date is now 4 months past. The current Aug 2026 close is a pushed placeholder with no confirmed path. The 30 Mar buyer email discussed a revised 2-phase proposal tied to a 31 Mar LOI that did not close. The buyer has been silent for 99 days since that email, and the rep's 2 Jul outreach has not yielded a buyer reply or next meeting. Without a forcing event or confirmed next step, the Aug close is not credible.

Champion (Siti) has gone quiet since 30 Mar 2026 (99 days ago, last buyer email; last call 9 Mar, 4 months ago). The 2 Jul Next Step does not mention her, instead naming a group-level stakeholder working budget confirmation, suggesting the locus of control may have shifted or she is blocked internally. Without champion re-engagement, the deal cannot restart momentum.

north_star_verdict.math: 54 days to close (30 Aug 2026 vs today 7 Jul 2026). Stage Formal Evaluation since the 17 Mar 2026 S2P proposal submission (~112 days in stage). The original LOI/MSA target (31 Mar/7 Apr 2026) lapsed roughly 13 weeks ago with no signature. The 2 Jul 2026 Next Step promises a budget-tolerance response 'in 1-2 weeks' (due ~16 Jul 2026), leaving under 6 weeks from that point to the 30 Aug close for finalization, contracting, and signature across a 7-port consolidation — an aggressive, unconfirmed pace.

north_star_verdict.headline: Partner-relayed proposal reportedly reached the MMC group owner with a positive response, but Zycus has had no independently verified buyer contact in over 90 days and the deal is stalled awaiting group-wide budget tolerance confirmation.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline, with close date pushed beyond Aug 2026 pending confirmed group-wide budget tolerance and direct economic-buyer engagement.

flags: No quantified value case built — the buyer has articulated qualitative pain (manual tendering, no e-bidding) but no ROI, savings target, cycle-time reduction, or headcount impact has been captured. On a $650k-$1M deal (7-port consolidation), the EB will demand a business case to justify the budget. (Gap: quantified metrics and ROI; move: co-author a value case with Siti and finance, quantifying the cost of manual tendering and the savings/efficiency gain from Zycus S2P + AI, and use it to re-engage the EB.)

Buyer flip-flopped on Ramco replacement, signaling unresolved internal alignment — the 9 Feb Next Step states mgmt decided to drop Ramco, but the 29 Jan Next Step says mgmt is looking to retain Ramco P2P short-term, and the 20 Jan Next Step records 'Ramco pushing them hard.' The buyer's indecision and 3-month silence suggest Ramco may have regained ground or the decision is stalled. (Risk: Ramco retention; move: re-engage to confirm the Ramco replacement decision, validate the 7-port consolidation scope, and push the EB conversation to lock budget before Ramco's renewal cycle closes the window.)

Secure a direct Zycus-side introduction to the MMC group owner's office or the group-level budget/finance stakeholder rather than relying on a third-hand proposal hand-off, since no Zycus rep has been on a call with either party.

Re-verify Ramco's current incumbent status before assuming displacement is locked in; the buyer flip-flopped on retaining vs. dropping Ramco as recently as Feb 2026 and Ramco was actively pushing its renewal.

Reconfirm Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus's role and access now that the decision has escalated above Northport to the MMC group level, since she has not been referenced in the deal record since it moved to the group entry.
Run a fresh Ramco competitive review before any group-wide commitment: Ramco actively pushed Northport in Jan 2026 and management flip-flopped on retaining it, with no resolution confirmed since.
best_practice_check.summary: Three levers matter most now: protect the original champion as the deal escalates, force a competitive review given Ramco's active push, and multi-thread the other 6 ports before the group-wide budget decision lands.

competitive_position.summary: Ramco is the single strongest current threat: it is the incumbent P2P system, has actively pushed back against replacement, and the buyer's own stance on retaining vs. dropping it flip-flopped as recently as Feb 2026 with no confirmed resolution since. SAP Ariba and iValua are listed on the Competitors__c field but appear in no call, Next_Step, or task evidence found this sweep; they should be treated as unconfirmed field-only entries pending corroboration.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A Formal Evaluation deal that has expanded from a single-port ~$240k proposal into a 7-port MMC group consolidation worth roughly $1M, with a positive but rep-reported signal from the group owner, while the buyer side has had no direct two-way call or email with Zycus in over three months and group budget confirmation and direct economic-buyer engagement remain outstanding.

deal_risk.text: Group-wide budget tolerance across all 7 MMC ports is unconfirmed as of 2 Jul 2026, and the deal (booked at $650,000 in Salesforce) cannot close at its likely true group-wide value (~$1.05M at ~$150k/port x 7) without it.

Decision authority has moved from the Northport buying committee up to the MMC group owner and a group-level budget/finance stakeholder, an unengaged approval layer Zycus has not yet reached directly.

win_position.text: A Zycus-authored proposal reached the MMC group owner and, per the 2 Jul 2026 Next Step, received 'a positive response and level of interest,' indicating real interest at the top of the buying hierarchy.

The deal is genuinely multi-threaded at Northport: five named contacts (Siti Zalekha Baizura Jayus, Nazual Azma Reffin, Agilaan Praemkumar, Azman Mohamed, Aimi Syazreen Ab Murad) attended the 9 Dec 2025 in-person demo, and the champion secured internal management approval to proceed with Zycus in Jan 2026.

No Zycus-attended call or email has directly reached the MMC group owner, the group-level budget/finance stakeholder, or Northport's 'new CEO' — every positive signal from that layer is rep-reported via the Next Step, not independently verified.
The incumbent Ramco relationship remains unresolved: Northport flip-flopped between dropping and retaining Ramco between 29 Jan and 9 Feb 2026 with no confirmed resolution in the four months since.

deal_momentum.text: No two-way buyer email or call has been recorded since 30 Mar 2026 (about 101 days ago), and the most recent Next Step update (2 Jul 2026) describes the buyer side as still awaiting a reply on group budget tolerance rather than new direct contact.

The Next Step trail shows dated forward movement roughly every 2-4 weeks from Nov 2025 through Jun 2026 (discovery, F2F demo, proposal submission, group-level escalation) — the pattern of an actively-run motion, not a stalled one.
An original LOI/BOM signature target of 31 Mar/7 Apr 2026 lapsed without closing, and the close date has since been pushed to 30 Aug 2026 with no new confirmed forcing event.

customer_commitment.text: The champion secured internal exco approval to move forward with Zycus modules (29 Jan 2026 Next Step) and personally requested costing to bring to her CFO for both S2C and S2P options (5 Jan 2026 Next Step).

That same champion has not appeared by name in any Next Step entry, call, or confirmed email since 9 Mar 2026 / 30 Mar 2026, and the current group-level escalation makes no reference to her continued involvement.
